title: Verbascum serpenticola
intro: Verbascum serpenticola, also known as the serpent mullein, is a species of flowering plant in the mullein family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Verbascum serpenticola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with serpent mullein include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate respiratory issues and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Verbascum serpenticola is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Verbascum, commonly known as mulleins. The family Scrophulariaceae is a group of flowering plants that are now often classified as part of the family Plantaginaceae. The Scrophulariaceae family was once a large and diverse group of plants, but it has undergone significant reclassification in recent years. Verbascum serpenticola is native to the southwestern United States.
